evals.prompt: I need a function that takes a payment status and returns the appropriate badge color for our UI. The statuses are 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', and 'refunded'. Here's what I have so far in src/components/PaymentBadge.tsx but the switch just has a default that returns gray.
evals.expected_output: A function using exhaustive case handling (never check, explicit return type, or Record mapping) with no default branch that silently swallows new cases
assertions: Status values defined with as const and type derived via typeof, not a hand-written union
Return type is a specific union (e.g. BadgeColor), not string
No runtime default fallback value -- missing cases are compile errors only
Uses Record<Status, Color> or exhaustive switch with never check
Uses named export, not default export

evals.prompt: We're fetching user profiles from /api/users/:id and the response has name, email, role (admin/member/viewer), and an optional phoneNumber. Can you write the API client function in src/services/userService.ts? We're using axios.
evals.expected_output: An API client that uses schema validation (Zod or equivalent) at the boundary with types derived from the schema
assertions: Uses Zod or equivalent schema for response parsing
Type derived from schema (z.infer or equivalent), not a hand-written interface
Parsing happens at the API boundary function, not deferred to consumers
Uses named export, not default export

evals.prompt:
Can you review this TypeScript file and tell me what to fix?

File: src/utils/dataProcessor.ts
evals.expected_output: Review that flags all type safety violations and structural issues per the standards
assertions: Flags every any usage in the file
Flags the unsafe as cast
Flags the default export
Flags boolean positional parameters
Flags the non-null assertion (value!)
Flags the parallel type definition drift (role: string vs literal union)
Uses the confidence framework: findings categorized as high/moderate/low confidence
Maps findings to specific standard sections from the skill
Does NOT flag formatting or import order issues

evals.prompt: I have this React component in src/components/Dashboard.tsx that fetches data, transforms it, and renders a chart all in one 180-line function. The data fetching uses useEffect with a manual fetch call. Should I break this up? If so, how?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation to extract data fetching from rendering, with skill-informed type safety guidance applied to the refactoring
assertions: Recommends extraction of data fetching from rendering concern
Does NOT blindly recommend extracting everything -- applies judgment about when extraction adds unnecessary layers
Recommends boundary parsing (Zod/schema) for the fetch response rather than a type assertion or generic cast
Identifies type safety risks in the existing code as part of the refactoring rationale, not just structural concerns
Any new files or functions use named exports
